ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Off Calendar

Application For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-14-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for request for default judgment against Defendant Feng Re, A California Corporation, Defendant Feng 24th, LLC, a California Limited Liability Corporation and Defendant West Edge Halo, Inc. A California Corporation.

Dept.505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:18:05 a.m. and concluding at 10:08:52 a.m.(D505)

Thomas S. Lakritz, Esq. appearing for and on behalf of Petitioner City and County of San Francisco.
Carole Ruwartz, Esq. appearing for and on behalf of Petitioner City and County of San Francisco.
Defendant Feng Re, A California Corporation, Defendant Feng 24th, LLC, a California Limited Liability Corporation and Defendant West Edge Halo, Inc. A California Corporation not appearing.
Kurt Fuchs sworn to testify on behalf of the Petitioner City and County of San Francisco.

Plaintiff's Complaint for tax collection filed on Nov-05-2019.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant West Edge Halo, Inc. a California corporation and Defendant Feng 24th, LLC, a California limited liability corporation; mailed on Nov-09-2019; effectuated on Nov-19-2019.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Feng Re, Inc. a California corporation mailed on Feb-19-2020; effectuated on Feb-28-2020.
Default entered as to Defendant West Edge Halo, Inc. a California corporation, Defendant Feng 24th, LLC, a California limited liability corporation and Defendant Feng Re, Inc. a California corporation on Dec-15-2020.
Dismissal of Defendant Does 1 through 50 on Jan-09-2024.
Application of Default Judgment filed on Jan-09-2024.

Counsel to summarize the causes of action and to provide offer of proof.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibit/s for the court's review:
Exhibit 1: Excel sheet of the calculation of the interest

Calculation of the interest is discussed between Court and Counsel.

The Court takes judicial notice of the Exhibit C of the complaint: Notice of Deliquency.
The witness is called to testify.

The Court confirms from the witness that the declarations made and testimonies are true and correct.

Counsel Carole Ruwarts offers proof as to the ownership of the property.

Court renders judgment and reviews the proposed judgment.

The Court reads in to the record contents of the proposed judgment and takes judicial notice of the following:
Lines 1 to 5.
Counsel to provide offer of proof as to Line 6.
Line 7 to 20
The Court amends Line 21.

The Court finds that the Plaintiff has met their prima facie case. The Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against Defendants as follows:

Plaintiff is to recover from the Defendants:
Damages $195,390.00
Costs $1,660.50
Interest $60,840.00
Attorney's Fees $0
Total judgment $257,890.50

Court signs and executes the judgment this day.
